text.title: ADR Supersession Detection Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the adr-supersession-detection methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: ADR MUST follow MADR / Nygard template; free-form ADRs excluded from scan.
Two ADRs MUST be flagged candidate-supersession when evidence-anchor overlap ≥ threshold (default 60%).
rule.rationale: Lower threshold floods report with noise.

rule.statement: Final supersession decision MUST be confirmed by a human; the script reports candidates only.
Once confirmed, the newer ADR MUST link `supersedes: ADR-NNNN`.
rule.rationale: Lost links break the decision trail.

rule.statement: Detection run MUST be scheduled at least quarterly.
Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
